angband_n.d: Morgoth's realm of the North; Thangorodrim above the gate; hosts bred beneath.

angband_x.d: 'The borders of Dor-deloth ever increased southward': Dorthonion, the Pass, Hithlum's fences taken.
angband_x.c: Silm 18–20; HoME V List of Names

hithlum.d: Walled by Ered Wethrin and Ered Lómin; Mithrim and Dor-lómin within; Hador's Men federate.

gondolin.d: The Hidden City in the Echoriath ring; one army of 10,000.

dorthonion.d: The pine highland, sixty leagues west to east; lost in the Bragollach.

doriath.d: The Girdle of Melian about Neldoreth, Region and Nivrim; Menegroth its heart.

nargothrond.d: From Sirion west to Nenning and the sea; the Guarded Plain watched from the caves.

falas.d: The walled havens Brithombar and Eglarest and the western shore.
falas.c: HoME XI, Grey Annals, year 473 — the published Silmarillion carries no years

brethil.d: The forest between Teiglin and Sirion, held by Haleth's folk under Doriath's leave.

marches.n: The March of Maedhros & the East
marches.d: Himring, Aglon and Maglor's Gap; Himlad; Thargelion under Caranthir (to 455).

ossiriand.d: The Land of Seven Rivers between Gelion and the mountains; bowmen who refuse open war.

lindon.d: Ossiriand's remnant about the Gulf of Lhûn; the last high-elven kingdom, then Círdan's havens.

eregion.d: The Ring-smiths' land of holly; south border the Glanduin; destroyed by Sauron.

khazad.d: Greatest of the mansions of the Dwarves; gates shut through the war of 1697.

lorinand.d: The golden wood between Celebrant and Anduin; Amdír, Amroth, then Galadriel.

greenwood.d: All the forest under the Silvan king before the Shadow.

numdom.d: Lond Daer (Aldarion), Pelargir (2350, the Faithful), Umbar (2280, the King's Men).

mordor.d: The fenced Black Land: Ephel Dúath, Ered Lithui, Barad-dûr; Núrn's slave-fields.

arnor.d: 'All Eriador except the regions beyond the Lune and the lands east of Greyflood and Loudwater.'

arthedain.d: 'Between Brandywine and Lune, and the land north of the Great Road as far as the Weather Hills.'

cardolan.d: 'Between the Brandywine, the Greyflood, and the Great Road'; wasted by 1409 and the Plague.

rhudaur.d: 'Between the Ettenmoors, the Weather Hills, and the Misty Mountains, with the Angle'; fell to Angmar.

angmar.d: 'Lands on both sides of the Mountains'; seat at Carn Dûm; broken at Fornost.

gondor_pk.d: 'North to Celebrant and the eaves of Mirkwood; west to Greyflood; east to Rhûn; south to Harnen and Umbar.' Mordor desolate within, under watch.

gondor_lt.d: Anórien and the southern fiefs; Ithilien contested; Calenardhon ceded 2510.

rohan.d: Cirion's grant: Anduin to Isen, Limlight to the mountains; Isengard a Gondorian enclave.

shire.d: Grant of Argeleb II: Baranduin to the Far Downs; Buckland 2340; Westmarch S.R. 1452.

bree.d: Four villages at the road-crossing; oldest settlement of Men and hobbits together.

rivendell.d: The hidden refuge of the Bruinen valley.

thranduil.d: Withdrawn north of the Mountains of Mirkwood before the Shadow; halls on the Forest River.

dolguldur.d: The Shadow's hill-fortress in the south forest; razed by Galadriel after the War.

dale.d: The Mountain-kingdom and the town of the bowmen; ruined by Smaug, restored after Five Armies.

ironhills.d: Dáin's realm; the mailed veterans of the East.

isengard_s.d: The Wizard's Vale and ring of stone; unmade by the Ents into the Treegarth.

rhun.d: The Easterling steppes about the inland Sea. Bounds unrecorded.

reunited.d: Arnor and Gondor under one crown; the Shire a protected Free Land; Ithilien under Faramir.

eastlorien.d: 'The forest south of the Narrows' given to Celeborn at the partition of Eryn Lasgalen.

mordor_e.d: Emptied by the Last Alliance; Gondor's fortresses watch the passes until the Great Plague.

slices.label: Years of the Trees
ev: The Girdle of Melian fenced about Doriath
Nogrod and Belegost delved in Ered Luin; Khazad-dûm far east
Angband rebuilt upon Utumno's western outworks
World-shape: the marred symmetry after the Lamps; schematic only

slices.label: F.A. 65–455 — the Long Peace
ev: The Noldor's leaguer rings Angband; realms as charted in Silm 14
Gondolin full-wrought after 52 years of secret toil
The Edain enter Beleriand c. 310–330 and hold fiefs under the Elf-kings

slices.label: F.A. 455–583 — the Ruin of Beleriand
ev: Ard-galen burned: Anfauglith, the Gasping Dust
Nirnaeth 472; Falas 473; Nargothrond 495; Doriath c.502; Gondolin 510

slices.label: F.A. 587 / S.A. 1 — After the War of Wrath
ev: 'Northward and westward many lands sank'; the Gulf of Lhûn breaks Ered Luin
Lindon remains of Ossiriand; Himling and Tol Fuin stand above the waves
S.A. 32: Númenor raised for the Edain — far west, off this canvas

slices.label: S.A. 750–1690 — Númenor's Noontide
ev: Eregion founded 750; the Rings forged c.1500–90
Vinyalondë/Lond Daer: the great deforestation of Minhiriath & Enedwaith
c.1000: Sauron takes Mordor; Barad-dûr rises

slices.label: S.A. 1693–3319 — War and Downfall
slices.sub: to the Change of the World
ev: 1697: Eregion destroyed; Imladris founded; Moria's gates shut
3319 the Downfall: world made round; Bay of Belfalas filled at east and south; Pelargir left far inland; Tolfalas nearly destroyed

slices.label: S.A. 3320 – T.A. 2 — Realms in Exile
ev: Arnor: 'all Eriador except beyond the Lune and east of Greyflood'
Gondor about the lower Anduin: Osgiliath, Minas Ithil, Minas Anor
3429–41: Last Alliance; Mordor emptied for two millennia

slices.label: T.A. 861–1300 — Division & Gondor's Height
ev: 861: Arnor divided — Arthedain, Cardolan, Rhudaur; Amon Sûl the tri-point
c.1050: the Shadow in Greenwood; men begin to call it Mirkwood
1601: the Shire granted by Argeleb II (late in this slice)

slices.label: T.A. 1300–1975 — the Angmar Wars
slices.sub: plague and the fall of the North
ev: c.1300: the Witch-realm at the Mountains' head; 1409 Amon Sûl razed; Rhudaur absorbed
1636: the Great Plague — Cardolan dies, Osgiliath depopulated, the watch on Mordor ceases
1974–75: Arthedain falls; Angmar broken at Fornost; the North Kingdom ends

slices.label: T.A. 1980–2510 — the Wandering Days
ev: 1980–81: the Balrog; Khazad-dûm abandoned; Lórien's flight begins
2002: Minas Ithil taken — Minas Morgul; 2050 the kings end
1999 Erebor founded; 2510 Field of Celebrant — Calenardhon granted to Eorl

slices.label: T.A. 2510–2941 — Rohan and the Dragon
ev: 2510: the Oath of Cirion and Eorl; Isengard kept by Gondor (Saruman from 2759)
2770: Smaug — the Desolation about Erebor and Dale (healed after 2941)
2912: floods ruin Tharbad; 2941: Erebor restored, the Necromancer driven out

slices.label: T.A. 2951–3019 — the War of the Ring
ev: 2951: Sauron declares himself; Barad-dûr rebuilt; Mount Doom rekindled
3018–19: the fronts — Isen, Hornburg, Pelennor, Morannon, Dale, Lórien ×3, Mirkwood
3019: Barad-dûr and the Towers fall in ruin; Isengard becomes the Treegarth; Núrn freed
Umbar, Khand and Harad lie south beyond this sheet's edge

slices.label: The Fourth Age — the Reunited Kingdom
ev: Arnor and Gondor reunited under Elessar; Annúminas restored
Eryn Lasgalen partitioned: Thranduil north, East Lórien south, Woodmen between
Minas Morgul destroyed by decree; Ithilien the garden of the realm again
The Elves diminish and depart; the mallorn blooms in the Party Field

pins: Aldarion's Vinyalondë at the Gwathló mouth — UT Aldarion/App D [C]
the elf-haven near the Morthond–Ringló confluence — UT; Baynes list [C]
the two westward bends of Anduin — UT [C]
[I] Not in any primary text — a label on the published general map only; absent from the Baynes name-list UT enumerates. The Reader's Companion calls it 'presumably a stronghold named for Fram'.
the Old Púkel-wilderness between Isen and Lefnui — UT Drúedain [C]
refuge-forest of Minhiriath's natives — UT App D [C]
Amon Dîn to Halifirien along the north flank — LotR V.1; UT [C]
the texts set it 150 leagues north of Menegroth; map tradition compresses — Silm 10; HoME XI [C]

pois.b: Morgoth's hells beneath the towers of slag; the Gate 150 leagues from Menegroth.
pois.c: HoME XI, Grey Annals §36 (also CoH, Introduction); CoH intro

pois.b: The green plain burned to gasping dust in the Bragollach; the Haudh-en-Ndengin rose here.
Fingolfin's great fortress at Sirion's well; Fingon's muster-ground of the Nirnaeth.
The isle-tower of the pass; Sauron's werewolf-keep; Finrod died in its pits.
Turgon's white city on Amon Gwareth amid the Echoriath; 52 years in secret building.
Thingol and Melian's hall over Esgalduin, fairest king-house east of the Sea.
Finrod's hidden halls above Narog; betrayed by its own bridge.
The Haladin's wood between Teiglin and Sirion; Túrin's last stand-ground.
Mîm's bald hill; Túrin's outlaw hold; Beleg's grief.
The laughing pools under Ered Wethrin, defiled by Glaurung.
Turgon's first hall by the sea, where Tuor found shield and sword left by command of Ulmo.
Northern haven of the Falas, Círdan's walled city; fell F.A. 473.
pois.c: HoME XI, Grey Annals, year 473 — the published Silmarillion carries no years

pois.b: Southern haven of the Falas; fell with Brithombar.
pois.c: HoME XI, Grey Annals, year 473 — the published Silmarillion carries no years

pois.b: Refuge of the survivors of Gondolin and Doriath; the Third Kinslaying fell here.
Círdan's and Gil-galad's last refuge; Balar's pearls in Menegroth of old.
Maedhros' hill-fortress that held the Leaguer's east — and held after the Bragollach.
Caranthir's seat under Mount Rerir; Thargelion about it.
First dwelling of Bëor's and Marach's folk east of Celon.
Where Melian enchanted Thingol; Eöl's dark wood; Maeglin's birthplace.
Ungoliant's brood-haunted precipices above Nan Dungortheb.
Dwarf-mansion of the Firebeards; Telchar's forge-city; sacked Doriath and died at Sarn Athrad.
Dwarf-mansion of the Broadbeams; Azaghâl's masked warriors withstood Glaurung.
The Ascar-crossing where Beren and the Green-elves ambushed the treasure-laden host of Nogrod.
Beren and Lúthien's last dwelling, in Ossiriand among the Seven Rivers.
Where Denethor of the Green-elves fell in the First Battle; the Fëanorians' last hold.
The trackless wild south of Andram, between the two rivers.
Where Fëanor burned the white ships of the Teleri.
The mound of the Nirnaeth dead, alone green in Anfauglith; Rían died upon it.
Círdan's harbours on the Gulf of Lune; the Ring-bearers sailed hence.
The palantír that looks only West; Elanor's Westmarch beneath.
Chief town of the Shire; the Mathom-house.
Under the Hill: Bilbo's and Frodo's door, where two Quests began.
Brandy Hall of the Brandybucks; the conspiracy unmasked itself here.
Old Man Willow's valley; the house of Tom Bombadil eastward.
Wights out of Angmar walk the old Edain graves; the swords of Westernesse came hence.
At the meeting of the Roads: the Prancing Pony, and a man called Strider.
Watchtower of the North's great palantír; razed 1409; the Witch-king's knife, Oct 6, 3018.
Elendil's first capital on Lake Evendim; restored by Elessar.
The North-kingdom's last capital; the great battle of 1975 broke Angmar west of it.
The Witch-king's mountain-hold of Angmar.
Whence Golfimbul's orcs marched to Greenfields.
Troll-country north of Rivendell; Arador's death-ground.
Bert, Tom and William stood here till dawn found them.
'By Elbereth and Lúthien the Fair, you shall have neither the Ring nor me!'
Elrond's Last Homely House, founded S.A. 1697; Vilya kept it; the Council met here.
The ruined river-town at the great crossing of the Gwathló.
Bandobras Took struck off Golfimbul's head here, 2747.
Durin's awakening-place, revered and defiled; the orc-capital of the North.

pois.n: Framsburg [I] Not in any primary text — a label on the published general map only; absent from the Baynes name-list UT enumerates.
pois.b: Of old the Éothéod's town at Anduin's springs; Fram slew Scatha the Worm.
Thorin's company taken here; Bilbo found the Ring in the dark below, 2941.
The Eagles' ford-rock; the skin-changer's oak-hall east of it.
Radagast the Brown's dwelling at Mirkwood's edge.
Gates of stone over the Forest River; barrels rode hence to Esgaroth.
The town on stilts; Smaug fell blazing into the Long Lake, 2941.
The Kingdom under the Mountain: Thrór's, Smaug's, Thorin's, Dáin's; besieged 3019.
Girion's bell-town, burned 2770; reborn under Bard; battle before it, Mar 3019.
Grór's iron-delvings; Dáin's 500 came mailed to the Mountain.
Whence the dragons bred; Dáin I died at his door of a cold-drake.
Isildur fell among the reeds, T.A. 2; Déagol's find, 2463.
The Necromancer's keep — Sauron veiled, 1050–2941; thrown down by Galadriel.
The city of the trees; Galadriel's mirror; 'no shadow lay upon it yet'.
Kheled-zâram's mirror-mere; the great battle of 2799; the East-gate of Moria.
'Speak, friend, and enter'; the Watcher; the Bridge where Gandalf fell, Jan 15, 3019.
The cruel mountain that defeated the Fellowship with snow.
Celebrimbor's city of the Ring-smiths, destroyed 1697; holly still marks it.
The Anduin's great loops where Eorl crossed riding south.
Eorl's charge saved Gondor here, 2510; the Oath followed.
Eldest forest-heart; Treebeard's wellinghall; 'the Ents are going to war'.
Rómendacil's giants of stone fencing the north-way of Gondor.
The Breaking of the Fellowship; Boromir's last fight, Feb 26, 3019.
The mere of Dagorlad's dead faces; Gollum led them through, Mar 1-2, 3019.
Mordor's iron door between the Towers of the Teeth; the last battle, Mar 25, 3019.
Théodred fell here Feb 25; Grimbold's shield-wall Mar 2, 3019.
The unbreakable tower in Saruman's ring — drowned by Ents, Mar 3-5, 3019.
Helm's winter-siege 2758-9; the great battle Mar 3-4, 3019; Gimli's Glittering Caves.
Brego's golden hall under the White Mountains; Théoden's seat.
The refuge of the firienfeld; the Muster of Rohan; the Paths of the Dead open here.
Isildur's black globe where the Oathbreakers gathered to the heir at midnight.
Great haven of the Faithful (S.A. 2350); Aragorn took the fleet here, Mar 13, 3019.
The Swan-princes' sea-castle; named for the last king of Lórien.
Seven walls under Mindolluin; the Pelennor before it; the White Tree in the Court.
The old capital astride Anduin; its Dome held the master-stone; the war began here.
The Tower of the Moon taken by the Nazgûl, 2002; the dead city of the pass.
Shelob's stair and tunnel; the Tower where Sam bore the Ring and found Frodo.
The Ring was forged in its fire, and unmade there, March 25, 3019.
Sauron's fortress, founded c. S.A. 1000, rebuilt 2951; its fall shook the world.
Faramir's hidden refuge behind the falls of Ithilien.
The ship-shaped river-isle guarding Anórien; stormed Mar 10, 3019.
Mordor's slave-fields that fed the armies; given freed to the slaves by Elessar.
The eastern sea of the Wainrider wars; Dorwinion northwest of it.
Arvedui Last-king drowned here with the palantíri of the North, 1975.

p.q: 'He walked briskly back to Bag End... a very trying time.'
the Witch-king wounds Frodo on Weathertop [paraphrase, not a quotation]

p.l: Rivendell: the Council; the Fellowship (Oct 20 – Dec 25)
p.q: 'The Ring must be destroyed... and it must be found in the Shire!'
'Speak, friend, and enter.'
'Fly, you fools!'
'No blemish or sickness or deformity could be seen... he saw no shadow.'
the Fellowship is broken ['I will take the Ring' belongs to the Council of Elrond, Oct 25 3018]

journeys.note: The united Company, Bag End to the Breaking — dates per App B, The Great Years

journeys.n: Frodo & Sam to the Fire (Feb 26 – Mar 25)

p.q: 'They lie in all the pools... a fell light is in them.'
'They cannot conquer for ever!'
'Well, this is the end, Sam Gamgee.'

journeys.note: The Ring-bearers' road — App B day-dates [C]

journeys.n: The Three Hunters & the Grey Company (Feb 26 – Mar 15)

p.l: The chase over the Wold (Feb 27 – Mar 1)
p.q: 'Forty leagues and five you have measured... such a deed of the three kindreds!'
'Arise, arise, Riders of Théoden!'
'The Dead follow us... they are summoned.'

p.l: The Stone of Erech, at midnight (Mar 8)

p.q: 'Thus came Aragorn son of Arathorn... borne upon a wind from the Sea.'

journeys.note: Aragorn's road from the Breaking to the black ships — App B [C]

journeys.n: Gandalf & Pippin to the White City (Mar 5 – 9)

p.q: 'Shadowfax must bear me... I will carry you.'

journeys.note: The white rider's last errand — App B [C]

p.q: 'Arise now, arise, Riders of Théoden!'
'Death! Ride, ride to ruin and the world's ending!'

journeys.note: Six thousand spears by the Stonewain Valley — App B [C]

p.l: The Morannon: 'the realm of Sauron is ended' (Mar 25)
p.q: 'The Eagles are coming! The Eagles are coming!'

journeys.note: Seven thousands marched to the Gate — App B [C]

journeys.n: There and Back Again (2941, by month)

p.l: the East Road [Bree is never named in The Hobbit — inferred geography] (May)

p.q: 'Dawn take you all, and be stone to you!'

p.l: The High Pass: Goblin-town — the Ring found (July)
p.q: 'What has it got in its pocketses?'
'I am he that walks unseen... I came from the end of a bag.'

journeys.note: Bilbo's route — Hobbit chapters; 2941 per App B; months per the narrative [C]

journeys.n: Tuor's road to the Hidden City (F.A. 495-96)
journeys.note: Ulmo's chosen: Mithrim to Gondolin in thirty-seven days of autumn and frost — FoG (Last Version); UT, Of Tuor

p.l: The caves of Androth above Mithrim
p.q: 'He dwelt alone as an outlaw...'

p.l: Annon-in-Gelydh, the Gate of the Noldor (met Gelmir & Arminas)
Cirith Ninniach, the Rainbow Cleft — gulls, and the Sea's calling
Into hollow Nevrast — the first of Men to look on Belegaer
Vinyamar under Taras: the shield, hauberk and sword left by Ulmo's command
p.q: 'Thus Tuor put on the arms appointed for him'

p.l: Ulmo rises in the storm; Voronwë cast ashore — east under Ered Wethrin
Ivrin defiled — and Túrin passes, crying for Finduilas: the kinsmen's only meeting
p.q: 'they saw a tall Man... clad in black, bearing a black sword'

p.l: The Ford of Brithiach in the Fell Winter
The Dry River; the Seven Gates of the Orfalch Echor
Gondolin amid the white snow — 'the dusk of the eighteenth day of Hísimë'

journeys.n: Beren's road — the Quest of the Silmaril (F.A. c.464-66)
journeys.note: 'Among the tales of sorrow and of ruin... there is yet that which is fairest' — Silm 19

p.l: Dorthonion: the outlaws of Barahir; the ring saved
Over the Mountains of Terror — 'a journey no Man had dared'
p.q: 'through Nan Dungortheb... where Ungoliant's brood wove their webs'

p.l: Doriath: Tinúviel dancing in the hemlock-glade; Thingol's bride-price
p.q: 'Bring to me in your hand a Silmaril from Morgoth's crown'

p.l: Nargothrond: Finrod remembers his oath — ten companions
Tol-in-Gaurhoth: the duel of songs; Finrod dies for him in the pits
p.q: 'He met Sauron with a song of staying... and Finrod fell before the throne'

p.l: Angband: Lúthien's spell before the throne; the knife Angrist cuts one Silmaril
p.q: 'the fire of the Silmaril was in his hand'

p.l: The Hunting of the Wolf; the hand and the jewel regained; the second life granted
Tol Galen the Green Isle — the Dead that live, beyond all seeking

journeys.note: Master of Doom by doom mastered — CoH; Silm 21

p.l: Dor-lómin: the Dragon-helm's heir, sent away before the Easterlings came
Doriath: fostered by Thingol; Saeros' death and the flight from judgment
With the outlaws on the marches; Beleg finds him
Amon Rûdh: Bar-en-Danwedh, the Land of Bow and Helm — and Mîm's betrayal
p.q: 'Dor-Cúarthol, the Land of Bow and Helm'

p.l: Rescued on Taur-nu-Fuin; Anglachel drinks Beleg's blood by his own hand
Healed of madness at Ivrin's pools
Nargothrond: the Black Sword; the bridge that doomed the Doors
p.q: 'Mormegil, the Black Sword of Nargothrond'

p.l: Back through the Fell Winter to an emptied Dor-lómin
Brethil: Glaurung slain at Cabed-en-Aras; Níniel; the black blade's answer
p.q: 'Yea, I will drink thy blood gladly'

scale_note: Scale calibrated on Bag End to Rivendell — but that figure is NOT Tolkien's; he nowhere gives the distance. It derives from Fonstad's Atlas ('slightly more than 400 miles') and is a tier-3 reconstruction [T3]. Tolkien's own distances, usable as a check: 45 leagues Rivendell–Eregion; 102 leagues Edoras–Mundburg; 42 leagues Pelargir–the landings.
scale_caveat: Every distance this map reports rests on a scale calibrated from Fonstad's reconstruction, not from a measurement Tolkien gave. Treat all mileages as tier-3 [T3].
